Demographic Portrait of Sebastopol, 2010-2024

Author

Thomas A. Petroff

Published

June 17, 2026

Population Growth, 2010-2024

Code 
#########
# Author: Thomas Petroff, RA - HPRC
# Date created: 06/17/2026
# Date last edited: 06/17/2026
# Project: Demographic Portrait of Sebastopol

## DESCRIPTION ##
# This R script investigates demographic indicators for the City of Sebastopol, CA
# spanning from 2010 - 2024 using 5-year ACS estimates.

# census_api_key("YOUR_KEY_HERE", install = TRUE)
library(tidycensus)
library(tidyverse)
library(ggplot2)
library(gganimate)
library(gifski)

# 1. DEFINE VARIABLES
acs_vars <- c(
  # Total Population
  total_pop          = "B01001_001",
  
  # Race & Ethnicity
  nh_white           = "B03002_003",
  nh_black           = "B03002_004",
  nh_native_american = "B03002_005",
  nh_asian           = "B03002_006",
  hispanic_total     = "B03002_012",
  
  # Nativity & Citizenship
  pop_nativity_total = "B05001_001",
  born_in_us         = "B05001_002",
  naturalized_cit    = "B05001_005",
  non_citizen        = "B05001_006",
  
  # Housing
  housing_total      = "B25003_001",
  owner_occupied     = "B25003_002",
  renter_occupied    = "B25003_003",
  
  # Households
  total_households   = "B11001_001",
  married_couple_hh  = "B11001_003",
  one_person_nonfamily = "B11001_008",
  two_plus_nonfamily   = "B11001_009",
  hh_with_kids       = "B11005_002",
  
  # Poverty
  poverty_universe   = "B17001_001",
  below_poverty_line = "B17001_002",
  
  # Income & Brackets
  median_hh_income   = "B19013_001",
  inc_less_10k       = "B19001_002",
  inc_10k_14k        = "B19001_003",
  inc_15k_19k        = "B19001_004",
  inc_20k_24k        = "B19001_005",
  inc_25k_29k        = "B19001_006",
  inc_30k_34k        = "B19001_007",
  inc_35k_39k        = "B19001_008",
  inc_40k_44k        = "B19001_009",
  inc_45k_49k        = "B19001_010",
  inc_50k_59k        = "B19001_011",
  inc_60k_74k        = "B19001_012",
  inc_75k_99k        = "B19001_013",
  inc_100k_124k      = "B19001_014",
  inc_125k_149k      = "B19001_015",
  inc_150k_199k      = "B19001_016",
  inc_200k_up        = "B19001_017"
)

# 2. DEFINE YEARS
# Note: For ACS 5-year data, 2020 WAS released. We keep it in the sequence.
years <- 2010:2024

# Sebastopol City GEOID for Census Places in California
sebastopol_geoid <- "0670770"

# 3. GATHER DATA THROUGH THE YEARS
sebastopol_raw <- map_dfr(years, function(y) {
  message(paste("Pulling Sebastopol data for year:", y))
  
  # Core indicators
  indicators <- get_acs(
    geography = "place", state = "06", variables = acs_vars, 
    year = y, survey = "acs5", output = "wide"
  ) %>% 
    filter(GEOID == sebastopol_geoid) %>%
    mutate(yr = y)
  
  # Table B01001 (Total Age)
  age_table <- get_acs(
    geography = "place", state = "06", table = "B01001",
    year = y, survey = "acs5", output = "wide"
  ) %>% 
    filter(GEOID == sebastopol_geoid) %>%
    mutate(yr = y)
  
  # Table B17001 (Poverty)
  poverty_table <- get_acs(
    geography = "place", state = "06", table = "B17001",
    year = y, survey = "acs5", output = "wide"
  ) %>% 
    filter(GEOID == sebastopol_geoid) %>%
    mutate(yr = y)
  
  # Table B11001 (Households)
  hh_table <- get_acs(
    geography = "place", state = "06", table = "B11001",
    year = y, survey = "acs5", output = "wide"
  ) %>% 
    filter(GEOID == sebastopol_geoid) %>%
    mutate(yr = y)
  
  # Clean and isolate Estimate columns
  indicators_clean <- indicators %>% select(yr, GEOID, NAME, ends_with("E"))
  age_clean        <- age_table %>% select(yr, GEOID, ends_with("E"))
  poverty_clean    <- poverty_table %>% select(yr, GEOID, ends_with("E"))
  hh_clean         <- hh_table %>% select(yr, GEOID, ends_with("E"))
  
  # Base merge
  combined <- indicators_clean %>%
    left_join(age_clean, by = c("yr", "GEOID")) %>%
    left_join(poverty_clean, by = c("yr", "GEOID")) %>%
    left_join(hh_clean, by = c("yr", "GEOID"))
  
  # CONDITIONAL PULL FOR B05013 (Foreign-born Age Table)
  if (y >= 2014) {
    fb_age_table <- get_acs(
      geography = "place", state = "06", table = "B05013",
      year = y, survey = "acs5", output = "wide"
    ) %>% 
      filter(GEOID == sebastopol_geoid) %>%
      select(GEOID, ends_with("E"))
    
    combined <- combined %>% left_join(fb_age_table, by = "GEOID")
  } else {
    # Generate empty B05013 columns for years 2010-2013 so downstream mutations don't crash
    fb_cols <- setNames(rep(list(NA_real_), 39), paste0("B05013_", sprintf("%03d", 1:39), "E"))
    combined <- combined %>% mutate(!!!fb_cols)
  }
  
  return(combined)
})

# 4. FINAL CLEAN UP AND ISOLATION
# Clean up trailing 'E' from column names
names(sebastopol_raw) <- gsub("E$", "", names(sebastopol_raw))

# Create the final clean dataframe for Sebastopol
sebastopol <- sebastopol_raw %>%
  mutate(NAME = "City of Sebastopol") %>%
  select(-GEOID) %>%
  arrange(yr)


# CITY OF Sebastopol DATA MANIPULATIONS
df_analysis_sebastopol <- sebastopol %>%
  mutate(
    # ========================================================
    # 1. TOTAL POPULATION 5-YEAR AGE BINS (Table B01001)
    # ========================================================
    # --- MALE TOTAL BINS ---
    m_00_04 = B01001_003,
    m_05_09 = B01001_004,
    m_10_14 = B01001_005,
    m_15_19 = B01001_006 + B01001_007,                 # 15-17 + 18-19
    m_20_24 = B01001_008 + B01001_009 + B01001_010,     # 20 + 21 + 22-24
    m_25_29 = B01001_011,
    m_30_34 = B01001_012,
    m_35_39 = B01001_013,
    m_40_44 = B01001_014,
    m_45_49 = B01001_015,
    m_50_54 = B01001_016,
    m_55_59 = B01001_017,
    m_60_64 = B01001_018 + B01001_019,                 # 60-61 + 62-64
    m_65_69 = B01001_020 + B01001_021,                 # 65-66 + 67-69
    m_70_74 = B01001_022,
    m_75_79 = B01001_023,
    m_80_84 = B01001_024,
    m_85_up = B01001_025,
    
    # --- FEMALE TOTAL BINS ---
    f_00_04 = B01001_027,
    f_05_09 = B01001_028,
    f_10_14 = B01001_029,
    f_15_19 = B01001_030 + B01001_031,                 # 15-17 + 18-19
    f_20_24 = B01001_032 + B01001_033 + B01001_034,     # 20 + 21 + 22-24
    f_25_29 = B01001_035,
    f_30_34 = B01001_036,
    f_35_39 = B01001_037,
    f_40_44 = B01001_038,
    f_45_49 = B01001_039,
    f_50_54 = B01001_040,
    f_55_59 = B01001_041,
    f_60_64 = B01001_042 + B01001_043,                 # 60-61 + 62-64
    f_65_69 = B01001_044 + B01001_045,                 # 65-66 + 67-69
    f_70_74 = B01001_046,
    f_75_79 = B01001_047,
    f_80_84 = B01001_048,
    f_85_up = B01001_049,
    
    # ========================================================
    # 2. FOREIGN-BORN 5-YEAR AGE BINS (Table B05013)
    # ========================================================
    # --- MALE FOREIGN-BORN BINS ---
    fb_m_00_04 = B05013_003,
    fb_m_05_09 = B05013_004,
    fb_m_10_14 = B05013_005,
    fb_m_15_19 = B05013_006,
    fb_m_20_24 = B05013_007,
    fb_m_25_29 = B05013_008,
    fb_m_30_34 = B05013_009,
    fb_m_35_39 = B05013_010,
    fb_m_40_44 = B05013_011,
    fb_m_45_49 = B05013_012,
    fb_m_50_54 = B05013_013,
    fb_m_55_59 = B05013_014,
    fb_m_60_64 = B05013_015, 
    fb_m_65_69 = B05013_016,
    fb_m_70_74 = B05013_017,
    fb_m_75_79 = B05013_036,
    fb_m_80_84 = B05013_037,
    fb_m_85_up = B05013_038,
    
    # --- FEMALE FOREIGN-BORN BINS ---
    fb_f_00_04 = B05013_022,
    fb_f_05_09 = B05013_023,
    fb_f_10_14 = B05013_024,
    fb_f_15_19 = B05013_025,
    fb_f_20_24 = B05013_026,
    fb_f_25_29 = B05013_027,
    fb_f_30_34 = B05013_028,
    fb_f_35_39 = B05013_029,
    fb_f_40_44 = B05013_030,
    fb_f_45_49 = B05013_031,
    fb_f_50_54 = B05013_032,
    fb_f_55_59 = B05013_033,
    fb_f_60_64 = B05013_034, 
    fb_f_65_69 = B05013_035,
    fb_f_70_74 = B05013_039,
    fb_f_75_79 = B05013_036,
    fb_f_80_84 = B05013_037,
    fb_f_85_up = B05013_038,
    
    # ========================================================
    # 3. MACRO DEMOGRAPHIC GROUPS & HOUSING
    # ========================================================
    total_age_00_17           = (m_00_04 + m_05_09 + m_10_14 + B01001_006) + (f_00_04 + f_05_09 + f_10_14 + B01001_030),
    male_age_00_17            = (m_00_04 + m_05_09 + m_10_14 + B01001_006),
    female_age_00_17          = (f_00_04 + f_05_09 + f_10_14 + B01001_030),
    total_age_18_64           = (B01001_007 + m_20_24 + m_25_29 + m_30_34 + m_35_39 + m_40_44 + m_45_49 + m_50_54 + m_55_59 + m_60_64) + (B01001_031 + f_20_24 + f_25_29 + f_30_34 + f_35_39 + f_40_44 + f_45_49 + f_50_54 + f_55_59 + f_60_64),
    male_age_18_64            = (B01001_007 + m_20_24 + m_25_29 + m_30_34 + m_35_39 + m_40_44 + m_45_49 + m_50_54 + m_55_59 + m_60_64),
    female_age_18_64          = (B01001_031 + f_20_24 + f_25_29 + f_30_34 + f_35_39 + f_40_44 + f_45_49 + f_50_54 + f_55_59 + f_60_64),
    total_age_65_plus         = (m_65_69 + m_70_74 + m_75_79 + m_80_84 + m_85_up) + (f_65_69 + f_70_74 + f_75_79 + f_80_84 + f_85_up),
    male_age_65_plus          = (m_65_69 + m_70_74 + m_75_79 + m_80_84 + m_85_up),
    female_age_65_plus        = (f_65_69 + f_70_74 + f_75_79 + f_80_84 + f_85_up),
    male_pop                  = male_age_00_17 + male_age_18_64 + male_age_65_plus,
    female_pop                = female_age_00_17 + female_age_18_64 + female_age_65_plus,
    nh_other                  = total_pop - (hispanic_total + nh_white + nh_black + nh_asian + nh_native_american),
    foreign_born_count        = naturalized_cit + non_citizen,
    male_other_family_hh      = B11001_005, 
    female_other_family_hh    = B11001_006,
    poverty_child_count       = (B17001_004 + B17001_005 + B17001_006 + B17001_007 + B17001_008 + B17001_009) + (B17001_018 + B17001_019 + B17001_020 + B17001_021 + B17001_022 + B17001_023),
    poverty_senior_count      = (B17001_015 + B17001_016) + (B17001_029 + B17001_030),
    poverty_working_age_count = below_poverty_line - (poverty_child_count + poverty_senior_count)
  ) %>%
  
  # ========================================================
# 4. EXPLICIT SELECTION AND COLUMN ORDERING
# ========================================================
select(
  yr, total_pop, male_pop, female_pop,
  
  # Total Pop Pyramid Intervals (Preceding)
  m_00_04, m_05_09, m_10_14, m_15_19, m_20_24, m_25_29, m_30_34, m_35_39, m_40_44, m_45_49, m_50_54, m_55_59, m_60_64, m_65_69, m_70_74, m_75_79, m_80_84, m_85_up,
  f_00_04, f_05_09, f_10_14, f_15_19, f_20_24, f_25_29, f_30_34, f_35_39, f_40_44, f_45_49, f_50_54, f_55_59, f_60_64, f_65_69, f_70_74, f_75_79, f_80_84, f_85_up,
  
  # Foreign-Born Pyramid Intervals (Following)
  fb_m_00_04, fb_m_05_09, fb_m_10_14, fb_m_15_19, fb_m_20_24, fb_m_25_29, fb_m_30_34, fb_m_35_39, fb_m_40_44, fb_m_45_49, fb_m_50_54, fb_m_55_59, fb_m_60_64, fb_m_65_69, fb_m_70_74, fb_m_75_79, fb_m_80_84, fb_m_85_up,
  fb_f_00_04, fb_f_05_09, fb_f_10_14, fb_f_15_19, fb_f_20_24, fb_f_25_29, fb_f_30_34, fb_f_35_39, fb_f_40_44, fb_f_45_49, fb_f_50_54, fb_f_55_59, fb_f_60_64, fb_f_65_69, fb_f_70_74, fb_f_75_79, fb_f_80_84, fb_f_85_up,
  
  # Macro Structural Metrics
  total_age_00_17, total_age_18_64, total_age_65_plus, 
  male_age_00_17, male_age_18_64, male_age_65_plus, 
  female_age_00_17, female_age_18_64, female_age_65_plus,
  nh_white, nh_black, nh_asian, nh_native_american, nh_other, hispanic_total, foreign_born_count,
  housing_total, owner_occupied, renter_occupied, total_households, married_couple_hh, 
  male_other_family_hh, female_other_family_hh, one_person_nonfamily, two_plus_nonfamily, hh_with_kids,
  poverty_universe, below_poverty_line, poverty_child_count, poverty_senior_count, poverty_working_age_count,
  median_hh_income, starts_with("inc_")
)

# ANALYSIS
# Filter for start and end years to track the structural shift
sebastopol_summary <- df_analysis_sebastopol %>%
  filter(yr %in% c(2010, 2024)) %>%
  arrange(yr)

plot_sebastopol_growth <- function(df_5yr) {
  ggplot(df_5yr, aes(x = yr, y = total_pop)) +
    geom_line(color = "#004c6d", size = 1.2) + 
    geom_point(color = "#004c6d", size = 2) +
    scale_y_continuous(labels = scales::label_comma(), 
                       breaks = scales::pretty_breaks(n = 8)) +
    scale_x_continuous(breaks = seq(2010, 2024, by = 2)) + # Step by 2 for plot spacing
    labs(
      title = "Total Population Trend: City of Sebastopol, CA",
      subtitle = "5-Year ACS Baseline (2010-2024)",
      x = "Year",
      y = "Total Population"
    ) +
    theme_minimal() +
    theme(
      panel.grid.minor = element_blank(),
      plot.title = element_text(face = "bold", size = 14)
    )
}

# Render the plot
plot_sebastopol_growth(df_analysis_sebastopol)

Broad Population Age Structure, 2005 vs. 2024

Both Sexes

Code 
# --- Broad Age Breakdown for Total Sebastopol Population ---
table_seb_age_total <- sebastopol_summary %>%
  mutate(
    `% 0-17`  = round(total_age_00_17 / total_pop * 100, 1),
    `% 18-64` = round(total_age_18_64 / total_pop * 100, 1),
    `% 65+`   = round(total_age_65_plus / total_pop * 100, 1)
  ) %>%
  select(yr, `% 0-17`, `% 18-64`, `% 65+`)

knitr::kable(table_seb_age_total, format = "html")
yr % 0-17 % 18-64 % 65+
2010 18.9 65.7 15.5
2024 16.4 55.7 27.9

Separated by Sex

Code 
# --- Broad Age Breakdown Split by Biological Sex ---
table_seb_age_sex <- sebastopol_summary %>%
  mutate(
    # --- MALE PERCENTAGES ---
    `Male % 0-17`    = round(male_age_00_17 / male_pop * 100, 1),
    `Male % 18-64`   = round(male_age_18_64 / male_pop * 100, 1),
    `Male % 65+`     = round(male_age_65_plus / male_pop * 100, 1),
    
    # --- FEMALE PERCENTAGES ---
    `Female % 0-17`  = round(female_age_00_17 / female_pop * 100, 1),
    `Female % 18-64` = round(female_age_18_64 / female_pop * 100, 1),
    `Female % 65+`   = round(female_age_65_plus / female_pop * 100, 1)
  ) %>%
  select(yr, starts_with("Male %"), starts_with("Female %"))

knitr::kable(table_seb_age_sex, format = "html")
yr Male % 0-17 Male % 18-64 Male % 65+ Female % 0-17 Female % 18-64 Female % 65+
2010 22 65.7 12.3 16.3 65.6 18.0
2024 18 56.9 25.0 15.0 54.5 30.5

Population Pyramid - City of Sebastopol

Code 
# --- 1. Reshape Total Population for Pyramid ---
df_seb_binned <- df_analysis_sebastopol %>%
  select(yr, starts_with("m_"), starts_with("f_")) %>%
  select(-starts_with("fb_")) %>% 
  pivot_longer(
    cols = -yr,
    names_to = "group",
    values_to = "count"
  ) %>%
  mutate(
    Sex = ifelse(substr(group, 1, 1) == "m", "Male", "Female"),
    Age_Group = gsub("_", "-", substr(group, 3, nchar(group))),
    Age_Group = ifelse(Age_Group == "85-up", "85+", Age_Group)
  ) %>%
  mutate(Age_Group = factor(Age_Group, levels = c(
    "00-04", "05-09", "10-14", "15-19", "20-24", "25-29", "30-34", "35-39", 
    "40-44", "45-49", "50-54", "55-59", "60-64", "65-69", "70-74", 
    "75-79", "80-84", "85+"
  )))

# --- 2. Calculate Interpolated Median Age ---
calculate_median_age <- function(df) {
  df_agg <- df %>%
    group_by(Age_Group) %>%
    summarise(total = sum(count), .groups = 'drop') %>%
    mutate(
      cum_prop = cumsum(total) / sum(total),
      low_age = c(0, 5, 10, 15, 20, 25, 30, 35, 40, 45, 50, 55, 60, 65, 70, 75, 80, 85)
    )
  
  median_bin_idx = which(df_agg$cum_prop >= 0.5)[1]
  if(is.na(median_bin_idx)) return(NA_real_)
  
  L <- df_agg$low_age[median_bin_idx]
  W <- 5
  C <- if(median_bin_idx == 1) 0 else df_agg$cum_prop[median_bin_idx - 1]
  F_bin <- df_agg$cum_prop[median_bin_idx] - C
  
  median_age <- L + ((0.5 - C) / F_bin) * W
  return(median_age)
}

# Clean dynamic processing using reframe
seb_median_ages <- df_seb_binned %>%
  group_by(yr) %>%
  reframe(median_age = calculate_median_age(pick(everything())))

# --- 3. Prep Plotting Coordinates ---
# Adjusted scale axis limits (limits = c(-600, 600)) to beautifully map Sebastopol's small cohort sizes
df_seb_plot <- df_seb_binned %>%
  mutate(plot_count = ifelse(Sex == "Male", -count, count)) %>%
  left_join(seb_median_ages, by = "yr")

p_seb_pyr <- ggplot(df_seb_plot, aes(x = Age_Group, y = plot_count, fill = Sex)) +
  geom_bar(stat = "identity", alpha = 0.8) +
  geom_text(aes(x = 17, y = 300, label = paste("Median Age:", round(median_age, 1))), 
            check_overlap = TRUE, size = 5, color = "grey30") +
  coord_flip() +
  scale_y_continuous(
    labels = function(x) scales::comma(abs(x)),
    breaks = seq(-600, 600, by = 200),
    limits = c(-650, 650)
  ) +
  scale_fill_manual(values = c("Male" = "#004c6d", "Female" = "#de425b")) +
  theme_minimal() +
  labs(
    title = "Sebastopol Total Population Profile: {frame_time}",
    x = "Age Cohort",
    y = "Population Count",
    caption = "Data source: U.S. Census Bureau, ACS 5-Year Estimates"
  ) +
  theme(
    plot.caption = element_text(size = 10, face = "italic", color = "grey40", hjust = 0),
    plot.title = element_text(face = "bold", size = 16),
    panel.grid.minor = element_blank()
  ) +
  transition_time(as.integer(yr)) +
  ease_aes('linear')

# Render and compile Total Population Profile
animate(
  p_seb_pyr, 
  nframes = 300,        # Smooth out transitions
  fps = 15,             # Snappy frame rate
  duration = 20, 
  end_pause = 40,       # Holds final frame so viewers can process 2024 data
  width = 900, 
  height = 750, 
  units = "px", 
  res = 110,            # High-fidelity text scaling for HTML layouts
  renderer = gifski_renderer()
)

Race/Ethnicity

Code 
# --- RACE-ETHNICITY ---
df_analysis_sebastopol <- df_analysis_sebastopol %>%
  mutate(
    pct_hispanic = round(hispanic_total / total_pop * 100, 1),
    pct_nh_white = round(nh_white / total_pop * 100, 1),
    pct_nh_black = round(nh_black / total_pop * 100, 1),
    pct_nh_asian = round(nh_asian / total_pop * 100, 1),
    pct_nh_native_american = round(nh_native_american / total_pop * 100, 1),
    pct_nh_other = round(nh_other / total_pop * 100, 1)
  )

df_seb_race_long <- df_analysis_sebastopol %>%
  select(yr, pct_hispanic, pct_nh_white, pct_nh_black, pct_nh_asian, pct_nh_native_american, pct_nh_other) %>%
  pivot_longer(cols = starts_with("pct_"), names_to = "race", values_to = "percent") %>%
  mutate(
    race = recode(race,
                  pct_hispanic = "Hispanic",
                  pct_nh_white = "NH White",
                  pct_nh_black = "NH Black",
                  pct_nh_asian = "NH Asian",
                  pct_nh_native_american = "NH Native American",
                  pct_nh_other = "NH Other"),
    race = factor(race, levels = c("NH White", "Hispanic", "NH Asian", "NH Black", "NH Native American", "NH Other"))
  )

p_seb_race <- ggplot(df_seb_race_long, aes(x = race, y = percent, fill = race)) +
  geom_col() +
  scale_fill_brewer(palette = "Set2") + 
  scale_y_continuous(limits = c(0, 100), breaks = seq(0, 100, by = 20)) + # Broadened limit for Sebastopol's demographic signature
  labs(
    title = "Race-Ethnic Composition: City of Sebastopol",
    subtitle = "Year: {closest_state}",
    x = "Race/Ethnicity",
    y = "Percent of Total Population (%)",
    caption = "Data source: U.S. Census Bureau, ACS 5-Year Estimates"
  ) +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 14) +
  theme(
    legend.position = "none",
    plot.title = element_text(face = "bold", size = 16),
    panel.grid.minor = element_blank()
  ) +
  transition_states(yr, transition_length = 2, state_length = 1) +
  ease_aes('cubic-in-out')

# Render and compile Race Composition shifts
animate(
  p_seb_race, 
  nframes = 300, 
  fps = 15, 
  duration = 20, 
  end_pause = 40,
  width = 900, 
  height = 650,         # Slightly shorter aspect ratio works nicely for bar charts
  units = "px", 
  res = 110, 
  renderer = gifski_renderer()
)

Household Structure

Code 
# --- HOUSEHOLD STRUCTURE ---
seb_census_colors <- c(
  "One person, nonfamily"            = "#08306b", 
  "Two or more people, nonfamily"    = "#2171b5", 
  "Male householder, other family"   = "#6baed6", 
  "Female householder, other family" = "#999999", 
  "Married couple households"        = "#d9d9d9"
)

df_seb_hh_structure <- df_analysis_sebastopol %>%
  filter(yr %in% c(2010, 2017, 2024)) %>% # Synchronized 5-year step bounds
  rename(year = yr) %>%
  group_by(year) %>%
  summarise(
    across(c(total_households, married_couple_hh, male_other_family_hh, 
             female_other_family_hh, one_person_nonfamily, two_plus_nonfamily), 
           \(x) sum(x, na.rm = TRUE)), 
    .groups = "drop"
  ) %>%
  mutate(
    `One person, nonfamily`            = round((one_person_nonfamily / total_households) * 100, 1),
    `Two or more people, nonfamily`    = round((two_plus_nonfamily / total_households) * 100, 1),
    `Male householder, other family`   = round((male_other_family_hh / total_households) * 100, 1),
    `Female householder, other family` = round((female_other_family_hh / total_households) * 100, 1),
    `Married couple households`        = round((married_couple_hh / total_households) * 100, 1)
  ) %>%
  select(year, total_households, 
         `One person, nonfamily`, 
         `Two or more people, nonfamily`, 
         `Male householder, other family`, 
         `Female householder, other family`, 
         `Married couple households`)

df_seb_hh_plot_data <- df_seb_hh_structure %>%
  pivot_longer(cols = -c(year, total_households), names_to = "household_type", values_to = "percentage") %>%
  mutate(
    household_type = fct_relevel(household_type, 
                                 "Married couple households",
                                 "Female householder, other family",
                                 "Male householder, other family",
                                 "Two or more people, nonfamily",
                                 "One person, nonfamily"),
    label_color = ifelse(household_type %in% c("One person, nonfamily", "Two or more people, nonfamily"), "white", "black")
  )

ggplot(df_seb_hh_plot_data, aes(x = factor(year), y = percentage, fill = household_type)) +
  geom_bar(stat = "identity", width = 0.65, position = position_stack(reverse = TRUE)) +
  scale_fill_manual(values = seb_census_colors, breaks = names(seb_census_colors)) +
  geom_text(aes(label = sprintf("%.1f%%", percentage), group = household_type, color = label_color), 
            position = position_stack(vjust = 0.5, reverse = TRUE), size = 3.5, show.legend = FALSE) +
  scale_color_identity() +
  labs(
    title = "Household Structure Profile: City of Sebastopol",
    subtitle = "Distribution of domestic environments using 5-year rolling baselines",
    caption = "Data source: U.S. Census Bureau, ACS 5-Year Estimates",
    x = NULL, y = NULL, fill = NULL
  ) +
  theme_minimal() +
  theme(
    legend.position = "right",
    panel.grid = element_blank(),
    axis.text.y = element_blank(),
    plot.title = element_text(size = 14, face = "bold")
  )

Median Household Income

Code 
# --- REAL MEDIAN INCOME (CPI ADJUSTED TO 2024 REAL VALUE) ---
cpi_lookup <- tibble(
  yr = c(2010, 2011, 2012, 2013, 2014, 2015, 2016, 2017, 2018, 2019, 2020, 2021, 2022, 2023, 2024),
  cpi = c(218.056, 224.939, 229.594, 232.957, 236.736, 237.017, 240.007, 245.120, 251.107, 255.657, 258.811, 270.970, 292.655, 304.702, 313.200)
)

seb_median_income_table <- df_analysis_sebastopol %>%
  select(yr, median_hh_income) %>%
  left_join(cpi_lookup, by = "yr") %>%
  mutate(real_hh_income_2024 = round((median_hh_income * 313.200) / cpi, 0)) %>%
  select(-cpi) %>%
  arrange(desc(yr)) %>%
  rename(
    Year = yr,
    `Median Household Income (Nominal)` = median_hh_income,
    `Median Household Income (Real 2024 Dollars)` = real_hh_income_2024
  )

knitr::kable(seb_median_income_table, format = "html")
Year Median Household Income (Nominal) Median Household Income (Real 2024 Dollars)
2024 87684 87684
2023 99600 102378
2022 98185 105078
2021 89679 103655
2020 83856 101478
2019 82244 100755
2018 77204 96295
2017 65319 83461
2016 58036 75735
2015 57560 76061
2014 52326 69227
2013 53677 72166
2012 53975 73630
2011 60000 83543
2010 53008 76137

Broad Metropolitan Area View

What can the City of Sebastopol do for residents with this information in mind?

Understanding population movement at the broader metropolitan level can help planners better understand who is moving in and out of the region and how the pace of this movement varies from year to year. In knowing this information, the Planning Department at the City of Sebastopol can help prioritize certain services and develop measures for ensuring a long term supply plan of resource availability for residents.

Code 
# =====================================================================
# --- REGIONAL ANALYSIS: CITY OF SEBASTOPOL VS SONOMA COUNTY (MSA) ---
# =====================================================================

# 1. Independently pull Sonoma County data for the same timeframe
message("Pulling regional comparison data for Sonoma County...")
sonoma_county_raw <- map_dfr(years, function(y) {
  get_acs(
    geography = "county", 
    state = "06", 
    county = "097", # Sonoma County FIPS
    variables = c(total_pop = "B01001_001"), 
    year = y, 
    survey = "acs5", 
    output = "wide"
  ) %>%
    # FIXED: Select the estimate directly and inject the year 'y' dynamically
    select(total_pop = total_popE) %>% 
    mutate(
      yr = y,
      Geography = "Sonoma County (Santa Rosa MSA)"
    )
})

# 2. Bind directly with your existing Sebastopol object
df_regional_growth <- sonoma_county_raw %>%
  bind_rows(
    df_analysis_sebastopol %>% 
      select(yr, total_pop) %>% 
      mutate(Geography = "City of Sebastopol")
  )

# 3. Calculate growth comparison matrix
table_regional_growth <- df_regional_growth %>%
  # FIXED: Changed %in= to %in%
  filter(yr %in% c(2010, 2024)) %>% 
  pivot_wider(names_from = yr, values_from = total_pop, names_prefix = "pop_") %>%
  mutate(
    absolute_change = pop_2024 - pop_2010,
    pct_change      = round((absolute_change / pop_2010) * 100, 1),
    ann_growth_rate = round((log(pop_2024 / pop_2010) / 14) * 100, 2)
  ) %>%
  select(
    Geography, 
    `2010 Population` = pop_2010, 
    `2024 Population` = pop_2024, 
    `Percent Change (%)` = pct_change, 
    `Mean Annualized Growth Rate (%)` = ann_growth_rate
  )

knitr::kable(table_regional_growth, format = "html")
Geography 2010 Population 2024 Population Percent Change (%) Mean Annualized Growth Rate (%)
Sonoma County (Santa Rosa MSA) 474047 485040 2.3 0.16
City of Sebastopol 7335 7445 1.5 0.11
Code 
# 4. Render the localized vs. regional comparison chart
ggplot(table_regional_growth, aes(x = Geography, y = `Percent Change (%)`, fill = Geography)) +
  geom_col(width = 0.4, show.legend = FALSE) + 
  scale_fill_manual(values = c("City of Sebastopol" = "#004c6d", "Sonoma County (Santa Rosa MSA)" = "#78a1bb")) +
  scale_y_continuous(labels = scales::label_number(accuracy = 0.1)) +
  labs(
    title = "Macro Population Growth Shift: Local vs. Regional",
    subtitle = "5-Year ACS Baseline Boundary Evaluation (2010-2024)",
    x = NULL,
    y = "Total Percent Change (%)",
    caption = "Data source: U.S. Census Bureau, ACS 5-Year Estimates"
  ) +
  theme_minimal() +
  theme(
    plot.title = element_text(face = "bold", size = 14),
    axis.text.x = element_text(face = "bold", size = 11),
    panel.grid.minor = element_blank()
  )
